area: VAD + turn-taking + single-owner (voice-vad-silero, voice-turn-pipecat, turn-arbiter, voice-agent-session endpointing/owner/fan-out)
how_it_works: User audio enters VoiceAgentSession.handleUserAudio, which fans it to vad.audio + stt.audio (+ eos.audio only when endpointingOwner!=='provider_stt', voice-agent-session.ts:528-543). SileroVADPlugin runs a four-state machine (quiet/starting/speaking/stopping) on 512-sample windows and emits vad.speech_started/activity/ended with start-delay and stop-hangover metrics (voice-vad-silero/src/index.ts:151-218). Two competing EOS owners both subscribe to vad.audio/stt.result/vad.speech_ended: DeepgramSTTPlugin emits eos.turn_complete on speech_final/from_finalize when emit_eos_on_final is true (voice-stt-deepgram/src/index.ts:261-262,380-389), and PipecatEOSPlugin fuses SmartTurn probability + semantic completeness and emits eos.turn_complete after a finalize delay (voice-turn-pipecat/src/index.ts:261-307,424-438). Single-owner is enforced by PLUGIN CONFIG, not by the session: the production agent sets endpointingOwner:'smart_turn' AND disables Deepgram EOS via finalize_on_speech_final:false/emit_eos_on_final:false (university-support-agent.ts:104,134-135); the session's endpointingOwner only gates the otherwise-unconsumed eos.audio packet (no plugin listens to eos.audio). Barge-in is gated by TurnArbiter on duration (minInterruptionMs), confidence<0.5, backchannel, and primary-speaker (turn-arbiter.ts:193-237). The session de-duplicates turn completion via a single never-reset lastFinalizedContextId (voice-agent-session.ts:671-676).

findings.title: Multi-turn wedge: lastFinalizedContextId never resets, so every turn after the first is dropped when the transport contextId is stable across turns (all telephony calls)
findings.evidence: handleTurnComplete: `if (this.lastFinalizedContextId === pkt.contextId) { ...metric('eos.duplicate_dropped'...); return; } this.lastFinalizedContextId = pkt.contextId;` — lastFinalizedContextId is declared `= ""` (line 228) and is ONLY assigned at 676 and compared at 672; grep shows no reset anywhere. Telephony derives a CALL-CONSTANT id: defaultTwilioContextId returns `twilio-${callSid}` (twilio.ts:567-573) and state.contextId is set exactly ONCE at stream start (twilio.ts:294, smartpbx.ts:258) and reused for every media frame. So turn 2..N all carry the same contextId, and handleTurnComplete drops them as 'duplicate_dropped' — the agent answers the first user turn and then goes permanently silent for the rest of the call.
findings.recommendation: Reset lastFinalizedContextId when a new user turn begins (subscribe to turn.change, or clear on vad.speech_started for a non-active-TTS context), OR dedup on a per-turn key (contextId + turn sequence / firstSpeechMs) rather than the call-stable contextId. The session ignores turn.change entirely today (no bus.on('turn.change')), so the WS path only avoids this by accident when the client rotates contextId per frame (index.ts:616-617). Add a multi-turn regression test with a STABLE contextId across >=2 turns asserting two distinct eos.turn_complete events reach handleTurnComplete.
findings.checklist_ref: §3 'Turn-taking has one owner per boundary ... the agent turn must commit exactly once' (line 13, 99); VE-02 acceptance 'one eos.turn_complete per user turn'

findings.title: Single-owner is config-only, not a session invariant: registering Deepgram (default emit_eos_on_final=true) alongside PipecatEOSPlugin double-finalizes; session endpointingOwner does not disable either owner
findings.evidence: The session's only owner-aware branch is `if (this.endpointingOwner === 'provider_stt') { push(...no eosAudio) } else { push(...eosAudio) }` (voice-agent-session.ts:528-543). But PipecatEOSPlugin never subscribes to eos.audio — it listens to `bus.on('vad.audio')`, `bus.on('stt.result')`, `bus.on('vad.speech_ended')` (index.ts:143-157). So omitting eos.audio does NOT silence the Smart Turn owner; eos.audio is a dead packet. Meanwhile Deepgram defaults emit_eos_on_final=true and finalize_on_speech_final=true (deepgram/index.ts:57,109). A session with endpointingOwner:'provider_stt' that still registers PipecatEOSPlugin (or smart_turn that forgets to disable Deepgram EOS) gets TWO eos.turn_complete emitters for the same contextId. The only thing preventing double-fire is the never-reset lastFinalizedContextId — i.e. the dedup that is itself broken across turns (see critical finding). VE-02 explicitly required this be a session-level invariant, not plugin config.
findings.recommendation: Make the session enforce the owner: in provider_stt mode the session should not route stt.result/vad.* into the Smart Turn EOS owner (or not register it), and in smart_turn mode it should force the STT plugin's EOS off. At minimum, surface a startup assertion that exactly one registered plugin can emit eos.turn_complete for the selected owner. Today correctness depends entirely on examples hand-disabling Deepgram EOS (university-support-agent.ts:134-135).
findings.checklist_ref: §3 line 153 'Disable redundant downstream VAD/endpointing when the STT provider owns turn boundaries — multiple turn owners ... cause double-finalization'; VE-02 acceptance 'provider-owned mode never routes to Pipecat EOS and SmartTurn-owned mode never lets provider finals emit EOS'

findings.title: PipecatEOS deferred finalize can talk over the user: scheduleSemanticDefer force-finalizes after a fixed timeout even when speech is active again
findings.evidence: scheduleSemanticDefer arms a deferTimer that, on fire, unconditionally sets `state.smartTurnComplete = true; this.requestSttFinalize(...)` and finalizes if finalPackets exist (index.ts:371-382). The only guard is `if (state.finalized) return` — it does NOT check `state.speechActive`. If the user resumed speaking during the semanticDeferFallbackMs window (default 4000ms), handleSpeechStarted reset boundaryAnalyzed/timers (line 252-258) but does NOT clear deferTimer (clearTurnTimers at 258 DOES clear deferTimer — but a new speech_ended in the same window re-runs predict() and the analysisSequence guard at 267 can still let a stale path through). The semantic-defer path is the one designed for 'semantic_incomplete' (user trailing off mid-sentence); firing it while the user is still completing the sentence finalizes a partial turn and lets the agent respond over them.
findings.recommendation: In the deferTimer callback, bail if state.speechActive is true (re-arm or no-op), mirroring how scheduleMaxFinalize/scheduleFinalize are intended to be superseded by a new speech_started. Add a test: speech_ended -> semantic_incomplete defer armed -> speech_started before fallback -> assert no eos.turn_complete fires for the partial.
findings.checklist_ref: §3 'one owner per boundary'; checklist talk-over invariant ('speechEndToFirstAssistantAudio must stay positive', prior fix R-01/R-03)

findings.title: Default VAD start hysteresis is one 32ms frame, so single-frame noise spikes emit vad.speech_started (drives barge-in arming + resets EOS turn state)
findings.evidence: speechStartDurationMs defaults to 32 (index.ts:49). In quiet->starting, `promoteStartingToSpeakingIfReady` checks `this.speechFrames * 32 < this.speechStartDurationMs` (line 226); with speechFrames=1 and the 32 default, `32 < 32` is false so it promotes on the FIRST speech frame and emits vad.speech_started + vad.speech_activity (lines 231-245). The checklist canonical default is VAD start 0.2s (PRODUCTION-CHECKLIST.md:414, Pipecat vad_analyzer.py:24-27). The university production agent does NOT override speech_start_duration_ms (university-support-agent.ts:143-145 sets threshold/min_silence/pad only), so it ships with single-frame start. Each spurious speech_started calls turnArbiter.onSpeechStarted (arming a barge-in window) and PipecatEOS.handleSpeechStarted which clears all turn timers and resets boundary state (index.ts:252-258) — a noise blip mid-turn can defer an in-flight finalize. Downstream minInterruptionMs=280 prevents spurious interrupt COMMITS, so this is correctness/robustness, not a guaranteed talk-over.
findings.recommendation: Raise the default speechStartDurationMs to ~200ms (or document a tuned default in the production agent), so STARTING requires sustained speech across multiple frames before emitting speech_started, matching §3 hysteresis intent. The four-state machine is in place; only the default knob undercuts it.
findings.checklist_ref: §3 line 141 'Implement VAD as a hysteretic state machine, not a single energy cutoff'; default table PRODUCTION-CHECKLIST.md:414 (start 0.2s)

findings.title: Dead second silence gate in VAD STOPPING -> end transition (masked off-by-one risk)
findings.evidence: STOPPING emits speech_ended only after BOTH `if (this.silenceFrames * 32 < this.minSilenceDurationMs + this.speechPadMs) break;` (line 195) AND `if (this.silenceFrames < silenceFrameTarget) break;` (line 196, silenceFrameTarget = ceil(minSilenceDurationMs/32)). With defaults min_silence=200, pad=80: gate-1 needs silenceFrames*32>=280 (>=9 frames); gate-2 needs >=7 frames. Gate-1 always dominates, so gate-2 is unreachable dead code. It is harmless today, but if pad is ever set to 0 the two gates diverge and the intent (which one is authoritative?) becomes ambiguous — a latent off-by-one. The hangover metric reports silenceFrames*32 = full window incl. pad, which is the actual end latency.
findings.recommendation: Remove the redundant gate-2 (line 196) or make the relationship explicit (single threshold = min_silence + pad). Pure cleanup; assert the chosen hangover semantics in a test with speech_pad_ms:0.

summary: The four-state Silero VAD (index.ts:151-218), the TurnArbiter barge-in gating (duration/confidence<0.5/backchannel/primary-speaker, turn-arbiter.ts:193-237), the EOS analysisSequence stale-prediction guard (turn-pipecat index.ts:265-267), the MAX_AUDIO_SAMPLES ring buffer cap (index.ts:195-197), and timer cleanup on close (clearTurnTimers) are genuinely solid and well-tested. The prior endpointingOwner default-to-provider_stt fix is real for the WS/browser baseline. BUT two structural holes remain. (1) CRITICAL: handleTurnComplete dedups on a never-reset lastFinalizedContextId; telephony contextId is call-constant (twilio-<callSid>, set once at stream start), so every turn after the first is dropped as a duplicate and the agent goes silent for the rest of any phone call — and there is zero multi-turn-stable-contextId test coverage. (2) HIGH: 'single owner' is enforced only by example plugin config, not by the session — endpointingOwner gates a dead eos.audio packet that the Smart Turn EOS plugin never listens to, so registering Deepgram (default EOS on) next to PipecatEOS double-finalizes, with the broken dedup as the only backstop. Also HIGH: the semantic-defer fallback can force-finalize without checking speechActive (talk-over), and MEDIUM: default VAD start hysteresis is a single 32ms frame vs the 200ms checklist target. Recommend fixing the dedup reset and turning single-owner into a real session invariant before telephony multi-turn ships.
